response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior software engineer with strong full-stack and systems credentials, but the role is specifically a C++ developer position requiring 8-12 years of hands-on C++ with STL, multi-threading, message brokers, IEC 62443, and industrial automation context. The resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with the rest of the career centered on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS platforms. No evidence of industrial cybersecurity (SDL/IEC 62443), MQTT, or industrial protocols like Modbus/OPC UA.
strengths: Broad polyglot experience across web, embedded, and distributed systems with strong craftsmanship/TDD discipline
Prior low-latency, high-throughput backend work (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) handling millions of requests/sec demonstrates concurrency and performance instincts
Mentoring and teaching track record (workshops, dojos, Selenium course) aligns with the mentoring responsibility
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience — only ~6 months at NI in 2015; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No evidence of message-broker-based architectures, UML-driven design, or industrial on-prem platform delivery

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for core role
Strong general engineering seniority & systems breadth
